title: Derive purpose-specific keys with a KDF
description: Use HKDF or a context-bound KDF to expand or separate keys per purpose, rather than reusing one key everywhere or hashing it ad hoc.
body: Use when one secret must produce multiple keys or a shared secret must become a symmetric key. Do not reuse the same key for encryption and MAC and signing, and avoid deriving keys with a bare hash and no context label. Actions: run HKDF (extract-then-expand) with a salt and a distinct info/context string per key purpose so derived keys are cryptographically separated; never truncate a key below its required length. Validation signals include distinct keys per purpose in tests and reproducible derivation from the same inputs. Risk boundary: cross-purpose key reuse means a weakness in one use compromises others; treat shared keys across distinct purposes as a design defect and separate them.
